template: You are an expert QA/QC engineer responsible for preparing a comprehensive, field-ready Quality Inspection Checklist for {{work_title}} related to {{discipline}}, strictly for on-site inspection use during construction activities, in accordance with reference standards: {{ref_standards}}, project specifications, approved drawings, and site conditions described in: {{remarks}}. Generate a structured, practical, and hierarchical inspection checklist that breaks down {{work_title}} into clearly defined inspection stages: {{inspection_stages}}. For each inspection stage, organize the content as a nested checklist structure starting with the stage title, followed by logically grouped inspection categories (such as material verification, pre-activity readiness, execution checks, in-process controls, concealed works, dimensional checks, testing requirements, and final inspection where applicable). Under each category, provide a sequence of concise, field-verifiable inspection questions written in a Yes/No or Compliant/Non-Compliant format, ensuring they are directly usable by site engineers during inspections without interpretation. Each question must implicitly reflect acceptance requirements aligned with {{ref_standards}} and project specifications, and where necessary include reference to required verification evidence such as MIR, IR, test reports, checklists, inspection records, or calibration certificates. Ensure the checklist follows a strict hierarchical structure: Stage → Category → Inspection Questions, with clear indentation or labeling to reflect hierarchy. All inspection points must be practical, technically precise, non-redundant, and fully aligned with real construction workflow. Do not present the output in tabular format, do not flatten the structure, and do not include any explanatory text outside the checklist. The final output must be a clean, field-ready hierarchical inspection checklist suitable for direct site execution and QA/QC use.
